message:
feat(fcp-oauth): expose private OAuth 1.0a parsers via __fuzz module

Add a hidden `__fuzz` re-export module that surfaces the otherwise
private `parse_request_token` and `parse_access_token` body parsers
so they can be driven from a libfuzzer harness without weakening the
public API.

The module is `#[doc(hidden)]` and the entry points are renamed to
`parse_request_token_body` / `parse_access_token_body` to make the
"this is a fuzz seam, not a stable API" intent explicit. The
underlying functions remain `pub(crate)` and are reused unchanged.

Also adds the matching fuzz target,
`fuzz/fuzz_targets/oauth1_token_response.rs`, which uses the
`arbitrary` crate to generate structured + raw OAuth1 token-response
bodies (oauth_token / oauth_token_secret / oauth_callback_confirmed /
optional user_id and screen_name fields, plus a raw-bytes mode) and
drives both parsers. The harness caps body size at 16 KiB and field
size at 256 bytes to keep individual iterations fast and to keep the
exploration focused on parser logic rather than allocator pressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
